title: Study Agriculture in Europe: Explore English-Taught Programs at the University of Debrecen
summary: If you are passionate about sustainability, innovation, and global food systems, the University of Debrecen offers an excellent opportunity to pursue a career in agriculture through a wide range of internationally recognized programs taught entirely in English. Located in Hungary, this prestigious institution combines centuries of academic excellence with modern research and innovation, making it an ideal destination for students seeking high-quality education in agricultural sciences and related fields. Why Choose Agriculture as a Career Path? Agriculture today goes far beyond traditional farming. It is a dynamic, interdisciplinary field that intersects with business, environmental science, technology, and social development. As global challenges such as climate change, population growth, and food security intensify, the demand for skilled professionals in agriculture continues to grow. By studying agriculture, students gain the knowledge and tools needed to develop innovative solutions, improve food systems, and contribute to sustainable development. From smart farming technologies to environmental conservation, this field offers endless opportunities to make a real-world impact. Programs Offered in English The University of Debrecen provides a diverse selection of programs designed to equip students with both theoretical knowledge and practical skills. These programs cater to different levels of study, ensuring that students can find a pathway that suits their academic and career goals. Undergraduate Program Agricultural Engineering (BSc) Graduate Programs Animal Husbandry Engineering (MSc) Agricultural Environmental Management Engineering (MSc) Crop Production Engineering (MSc) Food Safety and Quality Engineering (MSc) Plant Protection (MSc) Each program is designed to address current industry needs and prepare graduates for careers in research, innovation, and agricultural management. Why Study at the University of Debrecen? The University of Debrecen is one of Hungary’s leading public research universities, with a history dating back to 1538. It is recognized globally for its academic excellence and strong focus on research and innovation. Students benefit from: A vibrant academic community of over 28,000 students A diverse international environment with students from more than 120 countries Access to over 500 research laboratories Strong partnerships with local and multinational companies Globally recognized degrees and competitive rankings The university is located in a unique natural forest area under international environmental protection, providing a peaceful and inspiring environment for study and research. Career Opportunities and Future Prospects Graduates from these programs are well-prepared for a variety of career paths, including agricultural engineering, environmental management, food safety, and agribusiness. The university’s strong industry connections also enhance employability by providing students with exposure to real-world challenges and professional networks. With agriculture becoming increasingly technology-driven, graduates can also explore opportunities in innovation, data-driven farming, and sustainable development initiatives worldwide. Application Process and Student Support Prospective students can apply online through the university’s official platform. The application process is designed to be accessible and straightforward, with detailed guidance available on program requirements, admission criteria, and deadlines. The university also provides comprehensive information on accommodation options and the cost of living, ensuring that international students can plan their studies effectively. For those who need further assistance, the admissions team is available to answer questions and guide applicants through each step of the process. Conclusion The agriculture programs at the University of Debrecen offer a unique opportunity to gain a globally recognized education in a field that is critical to the future of our planet. With a strong academic foundation, innovative approach, and international environment, these programs are ideal for students who aspire to make a meaningful impact in agriculture and beyond. If you are ready to take the next step in your academic journey, consider applying and becoming part of a global community dedicated to innovation and sustainability. title: Pathways with Purpose 2026: U.S. Chamber Foundation Grant Driving Career-Connected Learning and Workforce Alignment
summary: The U.S. Chamber Foundation has officially launched its highly impactful “Pathways with Purpose” initiative, a funding opportunity designed to transform how education systems connect with workforce needs. This program focuses on strengthening alignment between K–12 education and labor market demands through employer-led collaborations, ensuring that students are better prepared for meaningful careers. With applications now open, this initiative invites employer-led consortia to design and implement innovative, scalable solutions that bridge the gap between classrooms and careers. Supported by the Pathways and Workforce Funder Collaborative, this grant represents a strategic investment in the future of education and workforce development in the United States. A New Vision for Career-Connected Learning The Pathways with Purpose initiative is built on the idea that education should directly reflect real-world workforce needs. Too often, students graduate without clear pathways into employment. This program addresses that challenge by empowering employers to play a central role in shaping education systems. Through strong partnerships with State Education Agencies (SEAs) and Local Education Agencies (LEAs), selected consortia will develop career pathways that align with in-demand jobs, industry standards, and evolving economic trends. The goal is not just to improve education outcomes, but to create a system where learners can confidently transition into sustainable, high-quality employment. What the Grant Offers Selected consortia will receive substantial financial support over a one-year period to design and implement their proposed initiatives. Beyond funding, the program provides a comprehensive support system that includes: Technical assistance and expert guidance Dedicated coaching throughout the project lifecycle Access to a wide network of industry leaders and resources Opportunities for collaboration and peer learning This combination of funding and strategic support ensures that grantees are well-equipped to deliver impactful and scalable solutions. Core Focus Areas All applicants are required to address two central pillars of the initiative: Aligning Curriculum with Workforce Needs Projects must demonstrate how they will use data-driven and employer-led approaches to align educational programs with labor market demands. This includes developing courses, credentials, and training pathways that reflect real industry needs. Expanding Work-Based Learning Opportunities Another key requirement is increasing access to hands-on learning experiences. These include internships, apprenticeships, job shadowing, and project-based learning opportunities that allow students to engage directly with the world of work. Applicants are also encouraged to propose innovative strategies that go beyond these areas, provided they enhance the overall goal of education-workforce alignment. Who Should Apply This opportunity is specifically designed for employer-led organizations that can demonstrate strong leadership and collaboration. Eligible applicants include chambers of commerce, industry associations, employer coalitions, economic development organizations, and other industry intermediaries. A critical requirement is the presence of formal partnerships with SEAs and/or LEAs, ensuring that proposed initiatives are both relevant and implementable within education systems. Applicants must also identify employer champions who will actively support and drive the project forward. Key Dates to Remember RFP Released: March 11, 2026 Applicant Questions Deadline: April 8, 2026 Applications Deadline: April 20, 2026 Selection Notifications: By April 30, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare early and take advantage of available resources such as the RFP overview, FAQs, and information session recordings. Selection Criteria and Expectations Proposals will be evaluated based on several critical factors, including organizational capacity, strength of partnerships, employer engagement, and the feasibility and impact of the proposed action plan. Selected consortia will be expected to demonstrate measurable outcomes, including: Development of new employer-aligned programs and curricula Increased access to work-based learning opportunities Higher attainment of industry-recognized credentials Additionally, grantees must commit to data collection, reporting, and active participation in a collaborative cohort environment. Long-Term Impact While the grant period lasts one year, the initiative is designed to create long-lasting, system-level change. By fostering strong collaboration between education and industry, Pathways with Purpose aims to increase the number of learners who can successfully navigate career pathways and secure meaningful employment. The program encourages innovation, urging applicants to push boundaries and develop forward-thinking solutions while ensuring that outcomes are measurable and scalable. Conclusion The Pathways with Purpose initiative represents a powerful opportunity for employer-led organizations to reshape the future of education and workforce development. By aligning learning with real-world demands, this grant supports the creation of sustainable career pathways that benefit both individuals and economies. Organizations ready to lead transformative change in education and workforce systems should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the April 20, 2026 deadline. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Pathways with Purpose 2026: U.S. Chamber Foundation Grant Driving Career-Connected Learning and Workforce Alignment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU Grant 2026: Supporting Civil Society Organizations in Sustainable and Inclusive Development through Global Gateway Investments
summary: The European Union has launched a major funding opportunity aimed at strengthening the role of civil society organizations (CSOs) in advancing sustainable and inclusive development across the East Africa region. Titled “Supporting CSO Role in Sustainable and Inclusive Development and in Global Gateway Investments” (Reference: EuropeAid/186336/DH/ACT/Multi), this initiative reflects the EU’s broader commitment to fostering participatory, transparent, and impactful development processes. With a total budget of 5,829,000 EUR, this action grant is designed to empower civil society actors to actively contribute to development strategies linked to the EU’s Global Gateway initiative. This flagship strategy focuses on building sustainable infrastructure, promoting digital transformation, enhancing climate resilience, and improving social systems such as education and healthcare in partner regions. Strengthening the Role of Civil Society Civil society organizations play a vital role in bridging the gap between policymakers and communities. They ensure that development initiatives are not only effective but also inclusive and responsive to local needs. Through this call, the European Union seeks to reinforce the capacity of CSOs to engage in policy dialogue, monitor development projects, and advocate for transparency and accountability. This grant specifically encourages organizations to take on a more active role in shaping and overseeing Global Gateway investments. By doing so, CSOs can help ensure that these large-scale projects deliver tangible benefits to local populations, particularly marginalized and vulnerable groups. Key Details of the Grant This funding opportunity is categorized as an action grant, supporting concrete projects that align with the EU’s development priorities in East Africa. The call was officially published on March 27, 2026, and applications must be submitted before the deadline on May 13, 2026. The grant is open for proposals targeting the East Africa region, with a strong emphasis on initiatives that promote sustainability, inclusivity, and participatory governance. Applicants are expected to demonstrate a clear understanding of regional challenges and propose innovative, impactful solutions. Priority Areas and Themes Projects funded under this call are expected to address key development priorities. These include promoting environmentally sustainable practices, supporting inclusive economic growth, and strengthening governance systems. Initiatives that focus on climate action, renewable energy, social inclusion, and community engagement are particularly encouraged. Another critical component is the integration of civil society into the monitoring and implementation of Global Gateway investments. This ensures that development efforts are aligned with local realities and uphold high standards of transparency and accountability. Benefits for Selected Organizations Organizations selected for this grant will gain access to substantial financial resources, enabling them to expand their operations and increase their impact. Beyond funding, this opportunity offers a platform to engage with international stakeholders, build strategic partnerships, and influence major development agendas. Participation in this initiative can also enhance the visibility and credibility of CSOs, positioning them as key actors in regional and international development efforts. This can open doors to future funding opportunities and collaborations. Application Process and Requirements Interested applicants must submit their proposals through the official EU submission platform. The process typically involves preparing a detailed project proposal that outlines objectives, expected outcomes, and implementation strategies. Applicants must also demonstrate their organizational capacity, previous experience, and ability to manage complex projects. It is essential to carefully review all official documents and guidelines before applying. Strong proposals are those that clearly align with the objectives of the call, present measurable results, and demonstrate sustainability beyond the funding period. Strategic Considerations for Applicants Given the competitive nature of EU grants, applicants should adopt a strategic approach when preparing their proposals. Aligning the project with Global Gateway priorities is crucial, as is demonstrating a strong understanding of local contexts and challenges. Applicants should also focus on building partnerships, particularly with local organizations, to enhance the relevance and impact of their projects. Clear monitoring and evaluation frameworks, along with well-defined indicators of success, can significantly strengthen an application. Conclusion The “Supporting CSO Role in Sustainable and Inclusive Development and in Global Gateway Investments” grant represents a significant opportunity for civil society organizations committed to driving meaningful change in East Africa. With substantial funding and strong institutional backing, this initiative highlights the importance of inclusive, transparent, and sustainable development. Organizations that are ready to scale their impact, influence development policies, and contribute to long-term transformation should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the May 13, 2026 deadline. title: Crack the Code to Join Downing Street: Inside the No10 Innovation Fellowship Hacking Challenge
summary: The UK government is taking an unconventional—and exciting—approach to recruiting top tech talent. Inspired by a long tradition of puzzle-based hiring, the No10 Innovation Fellowship has launched a hidden hacking challenge designed to uncover exceptional minds in AI, data science, and software engineering. If you’ve ever wanted to work at the cutting edge of public sector innovation, this might be your opportunity to stand out. What Is the No10 Innovation Fellowship? The No10 Innovation Fellowship is a prestigious program that places world-class technical talent at the heart of government. Fellows work directly on high-impact national projects, leveraging: Artificial Intelligence Data Science Software Engineering Cloud Infrastructure The mission is simple yet powerful: “Move Fast and Fix Things.” Participants don’t just build solutions—they help shape policies and deliver real-world impact at speed, working alongside senior decision-makers. The Hidden Hacking Challenge To identify standout candidates, the fellowship team has embedded a secret “Easter Egg” challenge within their official website. APPLY HERE. This puzzle is more than just a game—it’s a fast-track opportunity. According to insiders, solving it could help you skip part of the interview process . Once solved, candidates are invited to submit their solution via email: 📩 fellows@no10.gov.uk While success doesn’t guarantee selection, it significantly boosts your visibility among recruiters. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Why This Opportunity Stands Out Unlike traditional roles, the No10 Innovation Fellowship offers a rare blend of: Direct Impact : Build and deploy solutions used across government Policy + Tech Integration : Pair engineering work with legislative change Fast Execution : Work in an environment where ideas quickly become reality Access to Leadership : Collaborate with senior policymakers and experts Elite Peer Network : Join a cohort of highly skilled professionals This is not just a job—it’s a chance to shape the future of public service. Available Roles The fellowship is actively seeking talent across multiple technical domains: Forward Deployed Engineers – Deliver custom solutions to real-world problems AI Engineers – Build intelligent systems for decision-making Cloud Platform Engineers – Design scalable and secure infrastructure Frontend Developers – Create intuitive digital interfaces Open Applications – Exceptional candidates from any relevant background are encouraged to apply What They’re Looking For Successful candidates typically demonstrate: Strong technical expertise (Python, R, SQL, etc.) Experience in data science, consulting, or applied research Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills Ability to work in fast-paced, high-pressure environments A passion for improving public services through innovation Additionally, applicants must: Have the legal right to work in the UK Be eligible for Security Check (SC) clearance Deadline No official deadline has been specified. Applications and challenge submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early participation is strongly encouraged. Why You Should Apply If you’re driven by impact, innovation, and the opportunity to solve national-scale challenges, the No10 Innovation Fellowship offers a truly transformative experience. Whether you crack the puzzle or not, exploring this program could be your first step toward shaping the future of government technology. title: Global Remote Job Opportunity 2026: Apply for the Migration and Youth Platform (MYCP) Global Coordinator
summary: If you are passionate about global migration, youth leadership, and international policy engagement, the Migration and Youth Platform (MYCP) Global Coordinator role offers an exceptional opportunity to lead at the forefront of global advocacy. This is not just another remote job—it is a high-impact leadership position within a globally recognized youth-driven platform working closely with international policy frameworks and United Nations processes. This role is ideal for experienced professionals who are deeply committed to youth participation in governance, migration justice, and global collaboration. As MYCP continues to expand its influence in shaping migration policy discussions worldwide, the organization is seeking a capable and visionary Global Coordinator to steer its Secretariat and strategic direction. About Migration and Youth Platform (MYCP) The Migration and Youth Platform (MYCP) is a self-organized global network of children and youth (up to age 31). It provides a space for young people to actively contribute to international policymaking, particularly within migration governance systems. Key Highlights of MYCP: Established as a youth-led initiative advocating for meaningful youth engagement Anchored in global frameworks such as the Declaration of the Rights of the Child Actively collaborates with United Nations agencies and global stakeholders Plays a significant role in shaping: The Global Compact for Migration (GCM) The Global Forum on Migration and Development (GFMD) Conducts global consultations with youth across regions to ensure inclusive representation MYCP is widely respected for amplifying youth voices in spaces where decisions about migration and development are made. Job Overview: MYCP Global Coordinator The MYCP Global Coordinator is a senior leadership role responsible for overseeing the Secretariat and ensuring smooth organizational functioning. This individual will act as the central figure connecting internal teams, external partners, donors, and governance structures. Core Purpose of the Role: Provide strategic leadership Ensure operational efficiency Represent MYCP globally Strengthen partnerships and funding pipelines Key Responsibilities 1. Secretariat Leadership Oversee daily operations of the MYCP Secretariat Ensure organizational systems and workflows are efficient and effective 2. Team Management and Oversight Supervise project leads and support them in achieving their objectives Maintain accountability and alignment with MYCP values 3. External Representation Serve as the primary representative of MYCP in engagements with: UN agencies Global partners Donors and stakeholders 4. Fundraising and Organizational Development Identify and pursue funding opportunities Develop grant proposals and manage donor relationships 5. Governance and Board Coordination Liaise with the MYCP Board Prepare reports and updates for governance processes 6. Volunteer Coordination Oversee recruitment and engagement of volunteers Ensure well-being and productivity of volunteer teams 7. Reporting and Documentation Maintain accurate records Ensure compliance with donor and governance reporting requirements 8. Strategic Alignment Strengthen coherence across MYCP programs Ensure activities align with the platform’s long-term goals Required Qualifications To be considered for this role, applicants must meet the following criteria: Essential Requirements: Strong understanding of global migration governance, including: Global Compact for Migration (GCM) Global Forum on Migration and Development (GFMD) Proven experience in: Team management Project coordination Multi-stakeholder engagement Excellent communication and organizational skills Fluency in English (written and spoken) Proficiency in digital collaboration tools such as: Google Workspace Slack Zoom Shared document platforms Ability to work independently across multiple time zones Preferred (Desirable) Qualifications While not mandatory, the following will strengthen your application: Lived experience of migration or displacement Experience in: Grant management Donor relations Organizational development Background in youth-led or grassroots initiatives Experience working in Global South contexts Strong intercultural communication skills Prior involvement with MYCP in any leadership or coordination capacity Who Can Apply? Eligible Applicants: Professionals from any country worldwide Individuals with experience in international development, migration policy, youth advocacy, or global governance Candidates who have previously engaged with MYCP or similar organizations Target Group: Youth leaders and professionals (especially those familiar with youth-led initiatives) Policy experts in migration and development NGO professionals and international development practitioners Work Structure and Expectations Working Hours: 35–40 hours per week Schedule: Flexible but requires availability across time zones Travel: Occasional travel may be required depending on project needs and funding availability Work Environment: Fully remote with global collaboration Salary and Compensation Compensation is commensurate with experience and geographic location MYCP aims to provide fair and competitive remuneration aligned with global standards Why You Should Apply This role offers: A chance to influence global migration policy Direct engagement with United Nations systems Leadership experience in a global youth platform Opportunity to work with diverse, international teams A meaningful role in advancing youth rights and representation How to Apply Applicants must submit: A detailed CV A short statement outlining: Motivation for applying Relevant skills and experience Apply here Deadline 12th April 2026 The MYCP Global Coordinator role is a rare opportunity for individuals passionate about youth empowerment and migration justice to take on a global leadership position. With increasing importance placed on inclusive policymaking, MYCP continues to play a vital role in ensuring youth voices are not only heard but actively shape international decisions. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Global Remote Job Opportunity 2026: Apply for the Migration and Youth Platform (MYCP) Global Coordinator appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Taiwan AID Fellowship Program 2026 (Fully Funded) – Build Leadership in the NGO Sector
summary: The Taiwan AID Fellowship Program 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded international opportunity designed for passionate NGO professionals seeking to enhance their leadership skills, gain global exposure, and collaborate with Taiwanese civil society organizations. Organized by the Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID), this one-month immersive fellowship offers a powerful combination of workshops, hands-on NGO placement, and international networking. Why You Should Apply This fellowship is more than just a training program—it is a career-transforming experience that equips participants with practical skills and real-world insights into NGO operations in Taiwan. Whether you are involved in community development, education, or human rights, this program offers a platform to grow professionally and expand your global impact. Program Highlights 1. NGO Placement (3 Weeks) Participants will be matched with Taiwanese NGOs based on their professional backgrounds. During this placement, fellows will actively contribute to ongoing projects, including event planning, fundraising, and project management. 2. Capacity Building Workshops The program begins with intensive workshops focused on key areas such as project management, advocacy, and fundraising. Participants will also visit civil society organizations across Taiwan. 3. Roundtable Discussions Fellows will engage in knowledge-sharing sessions with Taiwanese NGO professionals, discussing trends, challenges, and innovations in the sector. 4. Future Collaboration Opportunities At the end of the fellowship, participants will propose project ideas aimed at fostering collaboration between their home countries and Taiwan. 5. Alumni Network Access Graduates of the program will join a vibrant alumni network, opening doors to long-term partnerships and regional cooperation. Financial Coverage This is a fully funded fellowship that includes: Round-trip international airfare Accommodation (primarily in Taipei) Local transportation within Taiwan Meals throughout the program Daily per diem during the NGO placement Program Timeline Arrival in Taipei: October 4, 2026 Workshops Site Visits: October 5 – October 8, 2026 NGO Placement: October 12 – October 29, 2026 Wrap-up Closing Ceremony: October 30, 2026 Taiwan LGBT+ Pride Participation: October 31, 2026 Departure: November 1, 2026 Application Process Applicants are required to submit: Updated CV and headshot One-page statement outlining motivation, expectations, and career plan Two recommendation letters Shortlisted candidates will go through a document review and interview process between June and July 2026. Final results and host NGO matching will be announced by mid-August 2026. Important Deadline The application deadline for the Taiwan AID Fellowship Program 2026 is June 1, 2026 (23:59 TST, GMT+8) . Late applications will not be considered, so ensure you submit all required documents before the deadline. How to Apply APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts If you are an NGO professional eager to expand your impact, gain international experience, and collaborate with like-minded changemakers, the Taiwan AID Fellowship Program 2026 is an excellent opportunity. With limited slots available, early application is highly recommended. title: UNICEF USA Paid Internships 2026: Open Roles, Salary, Eligibility, Benefits and How to Apply
summary: If you are looking for a paid internship in the nonprofit and international development sector , the UNICEF USA Paid Internship Program is one of the most competitive and rewarding opportunities available in the United States. This article provides a comprehensive, SEO-optimized guide covering currently open internship roles, salary, eligibility requirements, benefits, and the full application process. About UNICEF USA Internships UNICEF USA offers a limited number of paid internships each year for students interested in advancing their careers in global development, humanitarian work, policy, communications, and fundraising . Interns gain exposure to UNICEF’s mission, develop practical skills, and contribute to initiatives that support children worldwide. Internship Salary and Duration Hourly Pay: $18.00 per hour Duration: 12 weeks (Summer internships) Working Hours: Up to 30 hours per week Recruitment Cycle: Once annually (Summer intake) These internships are fully paid, making them highly attractive for students seeking meaningful and compensated work experience. Internship Location and Work Setup Internships are offered in remote or hybrid formats Candidates must be physically based in the United States throughout the internship Interns must have access to a reliable internet connection and a computer UNICEF USA does not provide relocation, housing, or travel support. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for a UNICEF USA internship, applicants must: Be at least 18 years old Be currently enrolled in a degree-granting academic program Have valid authorization to work in the United States Be able to complete the internship within the U.S. Important notes: Visa sponsorship is not provided International internships are not available under UNICEF USA Benefits of UNICEF USA Internships Interns receive a range of professional and developmental benefits, including: Paid work experience in the nonprofit and international development sector Access to a dedicated manager or mentor Opportunities to network with UNICEF professionals Participation in intern-specific workshops and organization-wide events Access to the Gallup CliftonStrengths® tool for personal development Potential eligibility for academic course credit The Internship Experience Interns at UNICEF USA are integrated into a structured and supportive environment. Key aspects of the experience include: Joining a cohort of fellow interns Participating in learning sessions and internal events Working on real projects aligned with UNICEF’s goals Building transferable skills in research, communications, advocacy, and program support Current Open UNICEF USA Internships (2026) Below is a list of currently open internship opportunities: Integrated Marketing and Events Intern Marketing Project Management Intern Digital Marketing and E-Commerce Intern Community Alliance for Child Rights Intern Foundation Partnerships Intern Corporate and Civil Society Partnerships Intern Major Giving Intern Brand Marketing and Communications Intern Global Policy and Public Affairs Intern UNICEF Data and Insights Research Intern UNICEF Next Generation Intern These roles span multiple functional areas including marketing, partnerships, policy, research, and communications. Other Open Roles at UNICEF USA In addition to internships, UNICEF USA currently has several full-time opportunities available: Assistant Director, Philanthropy Reporting and Analytics Community Engagement Associate (multiple locations) Manager, Philanthropy Reporting and Analytics Vice President, Corporate and Civil Society Partnerships Senior Project Manager, Integrated Campaigns These roles may be relevant for early-career and experienced professionals seeking long-term opportunities. How to Apply for UNICEF USA Internships The application process typically follows these steps: Submit an online application through the UNICEF USA careers portal Participate in a phone screening Attend virtual interviews via Zoom Receive a verbal offer if selected Receive a formal written offer Applicants are encouraged to apply early, as positions are limited and highly competitive. Equity, Inclusion and Organizational Values UNICEF USA is committed to fostering a workplace grounded in: Children and their Rights Big Thinking and Bold Action The Power of collective collaboration The organization prioritizes equity, inclusion, and belonging, ensuring that diverse perspectives are reflected in its workforce and programming. Important Considerations Applicants should be aware that: Interns are responsible for their own travel, accommodation, and living expenses Internship opportunities are limited and competitive Recruitment occurs once per year, typically for summer positions Why Apply for UNICEF USA Internships A UNICEF USA internship offers a strong foundation for careers in: International development Public policy and advocacy Nonprofit management Communications and marketing Research and data analysis Working with UNICEF USA provides valuable exposure to one of the most recognized organizations focused on improving the lives of children globally. Submit an online application through the UNICEF USA careers portal Conclusion The UNICEF USA Paid Internship Program 2026 is a highly valuable opportunity for students seeking paid, impactful experience in the nonprofit and international development sector . With competitive compensation, structured mentorship, and meaningful work, it stands out as one of the top internship programs in the United States. For more internships with UNICEF outside the USA, please CLICK HERE https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/2026/03/15/unicef-internship-programme-2021-fully-funded-internships/ The post UNICEF USA Paid Internships 2026: Open Roles, Salary, Eligibility, Benefits and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Join CLPR in 2026: Exciting Legal & Policy Career Opportunities in India (Senior Research, Research & Litigation Roles)
summary: The Centre for Law and Policy Research (CLPR) is inviting applications for multiple full-time positions in 2026, offering a unique opportunity for legal and public policy professionals to work at the intersection of constitutional law, social justice, and impact-driven research. CLPR is a not-for-profit organization committed to making the Constitution accessible and effective for all. Through legal research, strategic litigation, and policy advocacy, the organization addresses systemic discrimination affecting marginalized communities, including those impacted by caste, gender, disability, and minority status. With offices in Bengaluru and New Delhi, CLPR collaborates with academics, civil society organizations, activists, and policymakers to drive meaningful change through law and governance. Why Work at CLPR? Working at CLPR means contributing to impactful, rights-based initiatives while developing expertise in legal research, litigation, and public policy. Employees are part of a collaborative environment that values inclusion, diversity, and social transformation. Key benefits include: Direct involvement in constitutional and social justice work Opportunities to engage in high-impact litigation and policy reform Exposure to interdisciplinary collaboration with experts and institutions Professional growth in research, advocacy, and public engagement Inclusive workplace encouraging diversity and equal opportunity Available Positions 1. Senior Research Associate (Full-Time) Location: Bengaluru or New Delhi This senior-level role involves leading legal research, supporting litigation strategies, and contributing to advocacy and outreach efforts. Key Responsibilities: Conduct doctrinal, comparative, and empirical legal research Draft reports, policy briefs, and academic publications Support litigation and advocacy initiatives Coordinate with stakeholders and manage projects Contribute to communications and public engagement Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Law (mandatory) Postgraduate degree (preferred) Minimum 4 years of relevant experience Strong research, writing, and analytical skills Demonstrated commitment to social justice APPLY HERE 2. Research Associate (Full-Time) Location: Bengaluru or New Delhi An entry to mid-level role ideal for early-career professionals interested in legal research and policy advocacy. Key Responsibilities: Analyze legal materials and policy documents Conduct research on constitutional and social justice issues Draft publications and support outreach Assist in project management and stakeholder coordination Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Law 0–3 years of experience Strong writing, research, and organizational skills Interest in rights-based work APPLY HERE 3. Litigation Associate (Full-Time) Location: Bengaluru This role focuses on strategic litigation and legal aid, particularly in constitutional rights cases. Key Responsibilities: Draft petitions, affidavits, and legal documents Represent clients in courts, including High Courts and the Supreme Court Conduct legal research for ongoing cases Collaborate with senior advocates Requirements: Law degree and Bar Council enrollment Minimum 2 years of litigation experience Fluency in Kannada Strong courtroom, drafting, and analytical skills APPLY HERE Equal Opportunity Commitment CLPR is an equal opportunity employer. Applications are strongly encouraged from individuals belonging to marginalized and underrepresented communities, including SC/ST groups, minority religions, LGBTQIA+ individuals, and persons with disabilities. Deadline No specific deadline has been provided. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is highly recommended. Conclusion If you are passionate about constitutional law, social justice, and policy reform, CLPR offers a meaningful career path where your work directly contributes to societal impact. Whether you are an experienced legal researcher or an early-career professional, these roles provide a platform to grow while making a difference. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Join CLPR in 2026: Exciting Legal Policy Career Opportunities in India (Senior Research, Research Litigation Roles) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 in Singapore: Fully Funded Six-Month Opportunity for Researchers in Southeast Asian Studies
summary: The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 is one of the most prestigious research opportunities in Asia, offering scholars, historians, and advanced researchers the chance to immerse themselves in world-class archival collections in Singapore. Hosted by the National Library Board Singapore , this fully funded six-month residential fellowship provides unparalleled access to rare historical materials and contemporary archives related to Singapore and Southeast Asia. With a strong emphasis on academic excellence and impactful research, this fellowship is designed for individuals seeking to deepen their expertise while contributing to global scholarship in history, culture, and regional studies. About the Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship is a six-month residential programme based in the heart of Singapore . Fellows are required to conduct research using the extensive collections housed at the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and the National Archives of Singapore . These collections are among the richest in Asia and include: Historical materials dating back to the 16th century Early European accounts of Southeast Asia Rare maps and navigational charts of the region Publications from Singapore’s earliest printing presses Archives of prominent Singaporean authors Government and institutional records of historical significance The fellowship enables researchers to explore both historical and contemporary narratives , making it ideal for interdisciplinary and comparative research projects. Fellowship Deadline The application deadline for the Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 is: 29 May 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their research proposals and supporting documents well in advance to ensure a competitive submission. Financial Benefits and Support The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship offers a comprehensive financial package to support fellows throughout their residency: Monthly stipend for the full six-month duration One-time relocation allowance (for international fellows) Monthly accommodation allowance Reimbursement of a return economy-class air ticket This funding structure ensures that fellows can fully dedicate their time to research without financial constraints. Research Focus and Areas The fellowship is open to a wide range of research topics, particularly those that engage with: Singapore’s historical development Southeast Asian regional studies Colonial and postcolonial narratives Cultural heritage and archival studies Political, economic, and social transformations in the region Researchers are encouraged to design projects that make meaningful use of the library and archival collections. Eligibility Criteria The programme welcomes applications from: Curators Historians Academics Independent researchers Doctoral and postdoctoral scholars Individuals conducting advanced-level research Applicants must demonstrate a strong track record of achievement in their respective fields and present a well-defined research proposal aligned with the available collections. Fellowship Expectations and Obligations Recipients of the fellowship are expected to: Reside in Singapore for the full six-month duration Conduct research primarily using the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library collections Actively engage with the academic and research community Produce scholarly outputs based on their research The residency requirement ensures that fellows fully benefit from direct access to the archives and research environment. Publishing Rights and Academic Contribution Fellows retain the opportunity to publish their research findings, contributing to academic discourse and enhancing global understanding of Southeast Asian history and development. The fellowship also encourages dissemination through: Academic publications Conferences and seminars Collaborative research initiatives Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Academic and professional achievements Quality and originality of the research proposal Relevance to the available collections Potential contribution to scholarship and knowledge production The selection process is highly competitive, attracting candidates from across the globe. How to Apply Interested candidates must submit a complete application before the deadline, including: A detailed research proposal Academic CV Supporting documents demonstrating research experience Any additional required materials specified by the programme Applicants should ensure that their proposal clearly demonstrates how they intend to utilize the archival collections. Voices from Previous Fellows The National Library Board Singapore has previously awarded the fellowship to outstanding researchers, including: Nurul Kaiyisah Mohd Latip Muneerah Ab Razak Sophia Chen Zhiting Chng Xin Ying Jennifer Yang Han Song These fellows have highlighted the programme’s strong academic environment, rich archival access, and transformative research opportunities. Why You Should Apply The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship stands out as a unique opportunity to: Access rare and valuable historical collections Conduct high-impact research in a global hub Engage with leading scholars and institutions Advance your academic or professional career For researchers passionate about Southeast Asia, history, and archival work, this fellowship offers an unmatched platform for intellectual growth and contribution. title: 2026 Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships for Duke University Students
summary: The Samuel DuBois Cook Center on Social Equity at Duke University is now accepting applications for its 2026 Summer Research Fellowships. These fellowships are open to both graduate and undergraduate students and are designed to provide hands-on experience in cutting-edge research focused on social inequality. Participants gain the opportunity to deepen their scholarship, expand their professional networks, and actively contribute to the Cook Center’s mission of advancing social equity. The Cook Center conducts interdisciplinary research aimed at understanding the structures and systems that drive inequality in society. Its research pillars include educational and economic opportunity, health equity, carceral justice, and public policy engagement. The Center emphasizes a data-driven approach and methodological innovation, providing students with exposure to advanced research techniques and analytical tools. Fellows are integrated into dynamic research teams where they collaborate with faculty, postdoctoral researchers, graduate and undergraduate students, and other research professionals. Graduate fellows may mentor undergraduate students while simultaneously receiving guidance from senior scholars, ensuring a layered mentorship environment. This collaborative structure promotes leadership development, teamwork, and a comprehensive understanding of research practice in social equity. Why This Opportunity Matters Inequality remains a pressing challenge worldwide. The Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships provide students with the opportunity to investigate the causes and consequences of inequality and to develop evidence-based solutions. Through participation in this program, students gain valuable insight into how research informs policy, social programs, and public debate. The program emphasizes mentorship, collaborative research, and professional development, equipping students with the skills needed to succeed in academia, policy, and advocacy. Fellows gain practical experience, broaden their networks, and enhance their understanding of interdisciplinary approaches to social equity research. These experiences are crucial for students pursuing careers in research, public policy, education, and other fields focused on social justice. By fostering a diverse community of scholars, the Cook Center ensures that participants are prepared to contribute meaningfully to efforts aimed at reducing inequality and promoting social equity both nationally and globally. What You’ll Gain The Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships offer a wide range of professional, academic, and personal benefits: Stipend: Graduate fellows receive $10,625.01 for the summer term, while undergraduate fellows receive $2,500 per term, with the possibility of earning $5,000 if participating in both terms. Mentorship: Individualized guidance from faculty, postdoctoral researchers, and senior scholars provides valuable career support. Research Experience: Fellows gain practical skills by contributing to ongoing research projects, including data collection, literature review, analysis, and research dissemination. Skill Development: Build expertise in research methods, statistical analysis, interdisciplinary collaboration, project management, and communication. Networking: Engage with peers, mentors, and professionals across multiple disciplines, creating opportunities for future collaboration. Professional Exposure: Fellows share research outcomes through the Cook Center website and may gain visibility in academic and professional communities. Graduate fellows will also have the opportunity to mentor undergraduate fellows, fostering leadership and teaching skills, while undergraduates gain exposure to advanced research practices and interdisciplinary approaches to studying inequality. Eligibility Graduate Fellows: Must be current Duke University PhD students in good standing, enrolled for the Fall 2026 semester. Must coordinate with their department to ensure summer funding arrangements are compatible. Undergraduate Fellows: Must be current Duke University student enrolled for Fall 2026. Must be eligible to work in the United States. All Fellows: Must have a strong interest in social equity research and commit to active participation in fellowship activities. Must be able to attend biweekly team meetings and contribute to research projects for the duration of the fellowship. Application Process and Deadline Applicants interested in the 2026 Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships must download the appropriate application form for graduate or undergraduate students from the Cook Center website. All required materials, including completed application forms, curriculum vitae, statements of research interests, and letters of recommendation, should be compiled into a single PDF. Applicants will then submit their completed application according to the instructions provided on the official website. Deadlines: Priority consideration: March 31, 2026 Rolling applications: April 30, 2026, depending on availability To learn more and submit your application, visit the official Cook Center website .
